name: Baklava
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 13-inch by 9-inch baking pan.
2. In a large bowl, mix walnuts, sugar, and cinnamon; set aside.
3. In the baking pan, place one sheet of phyllo, allowing it to extend up the sides of the pan. Brush with butter or margarine. Repeat to make at least five more layers, sprinkling with 1 cup of the walnut mixture.
4. Cut remaining phyllo into approximately 13-inch by 9-inch rectangles if desired; otherwise, just ensure the edges are buttered for folding after adding the filling.
5. Preheat oven to 300 degrees.
6. Place one phyllo rectangle in the pan, brushing with butter or margarine. Repeat to make at least six layers, overlapping small strips if necessary, and sprinkle with 1 cup of the walnut mixture.
7. Repeat step 6 two more times, ending with the remaining phyllo on top of the last walnut layer.
8. Trim any phyllo extending over the pan's edges.
9. With a sharp knife, cut just halfway through all layers in a diamond pattern to make 24 servings.
10. Bake for 1 1/4 hours or until the top is golden brown.
11. Meanwhile, heat honey in a 1-quart saucepan over medium heat until hot, not boiling. Spoon the hot honey evenly over the baklava.
12. Cool on a wire rack for at least one hour before serving. Cover and leave at room temperature until serving time.
13. Finish cutting through the layers to serve.
